inline_comments.message:
There are unto 199 ports allocated for each deployment of onap what you are doing here will not cover the Part 1(described below) of nodePort allocation.

Below I listed nodeport allocation for 5 instance of ONAP.

1st instance: 30200-30399

2nd instance: 30400-30599

3rd instance: 30600-30799

4th instance: 30800-30999

5th instance: 31000-31199

Part 1. Here is sample nodePort for AAI, so as you can see this the following will match the regex with  the 3rd number being odd
 
hence the following regex will be used

sed -i-- 's/nodePort: [0-9]\{2\}[13579]\{1\}/nodePort: '"$4"'/g' ../$2/all-services.yaml

  21 spec:
  22   ports:
  23   - name: "aai-service-port-8443"
  24     port: 8443
  25     targetPort: 8443
  26     nodePort: 30233
  27   - name: "aai-service-port-8080"
  28     port: 8080
  29     targetPort: 8080
  30     nodePort: 30232
  31   type: NodePort
  32   selector:
  33     app: aai-service
 
 
Part 2. so in the following case that regex will match the other regex with the 3rd number being even.

hence the following regex will be used

sed -i-- 's/nodePort: [0-9]\{2\}[02468]\{1\}/nodePort: '"$3"'/g' ../$2/all-services.yaml
 
spec:
  42   ports:
  43   - name: "model-loader-service-port-8443"
  44     port: 8443
  45     nodePort: 30229
  46   - name: "model-loader-service-port-8080"
  47     port: 8080
  48     nodePort: 30210
  49   type: NodePort
